question: A modeler deploys an ad-hoc sub-process containing a start event, two service tasks `Task A` and `Task B`, and an end event. Which constraint does this model violate?

options.text: Ad-hoc sub-processes must have exactly one outgoing sequence flow from their start event.
Ad-hoc sub-processes must use a parallel gateway after the start event to activate inner elements.
Ad-hoc sub-processes must not contain start events or end events; only activities (and optional sequence flows between them) are allowed.
Ad-hoc sub-processes must contain only service tasks; user and other task types are not allowed.

a.text: An ad-hoc sub-process is not allowed to have a start event in the first place, so a rule about its outgoing sequence flow does not apply.

b.text: There is no rule that an ad-hoc sub-process must begin with a parallel gateway. Inner elements are activated externally, not by token flow from a start event.

c.text: The docs list two constraints: an ad-hoc sub-process must have at least one activity, and it must not have start events or end events. Inner elements are not connected to start or end events and can be executed multiple times, in any order, or skipped.

d.text: No task-type restriction is documented for ad-hoc sub-processes. The constraint is about start and end events, not about which kinds of activities may appear.

explanation: Ad-hoc sub-processes are a special kind of embedded subprocess marked with the `~` tilde. Their inner elements are not connected to start or end events; each element can be executed multiple times, in any order, or skipped. The two documented constraints are that the sub-process must contain at least one activity, and that it must not contain start events or end events.
